brief_document.title: Per-shard secondary stream index: O(streams) enumeration instead of O(total entries) full-tree walk
brief_document.purpose: EventStore::scan / stream enumeration currently walks the ENTIRE committed tree to discover which streams exist (shard/actor/scan.rs enumeration is O(total entries across all streams)). Maintain a per-shard in-memory secondary index BTreeMap<stream_key, next_seq> of live stream keys so enumeration is O(number of streams), not O(total events). This is the larger read-path win after PERF-001 (scan first-match, already landed). Behaviour-preserving: enumeration returns exactly the same stream set in the same order; only the cost drops.
brief_document.task: Add a per-shard secondary index (a BTreeMap<stream_key, next_seq> or equivalent) of currently-live stream keys, owned by the shard actor (single-threaded, so no locking needed). Maintain it at EXACTLY the points that mutate the committed set: (1) commit (the universal durable-write funnel), (2) from_recovered (rebuild on startup/recovery from the committed tree), and (3) merge_adopt / any path that swaps committed_root wholesale (this BYPASSES commit and is the load-bearing trap — the index MUST be rebuilt or reconciled there or it will silently go stale). Wire stream enumeration (the scan path in shard/actor/scan.rs and its Database/EventStore caller) to read the index instead of walking the whole tree. Do NOT change observable enumeration results or ordering, TTL/visibility semantics, durability, hashing, replication, or the epoch-fence/commit linearization.

requirements.title: Per-shard live-stream secondary index
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL maintain, per shard, an in-memory ordered index of live stream keys (stream_key -> next_seq, or equivalent) that reflects exactly the streams a full enumeration would return. It SHALL be updated on every committed mutation and SHALL be consistent with the committed tree after commit, recovery, and committed-root adoption.
acceptance: A per-shard ordered structure (e.g. BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, u64>) of live stream keys exists on the shard actor state.

It is maintained at commit (the write funnel), at from_recovered (rebuilt from the committed tree on startup/recovery), AND at merge_adopt / any committed_root wholesale swap (rebuilt/reconciled there).
A stream that becomes fully tombstoned/expired is removed from (or excluded by) the index so enumeration does not report it.
No locking is introduced for the index (the shard actor is single-threaded by construction).

requirements.title: Enumeration reads the index, not a full-tree walk
requirements.spec: Stream enumeration (the scan path) SHALL consult the secondary index instead of walking the entire committed tree, making it O(streams). EventStore::scan's observable output (the set and order of streams) SHALL be unchanged.
acceptance: The enumeration in shard/actor/scan.rs no longer performs an O(total-entries) full-tree walk to list streams.
Existing scan / enumeration tests pass unchanged.
A regression test asserts the index stays correct across commit, recovery (reopen), and a merge_adopt / committed-root swap (the trap): enumeration after each matches a from-scratch full walk.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change observable enumeration/scan results or ordering, or the public range/get/scan/append API.
SHALL NOT touch durability, hashing, replication, or the epoch-fence/commit linearization point.
SHALL NOT introduce unsafe code; the crate sets unsafe_code = forbid.
SHALL keep no production unwrap()/expect()/panic!(); the crate denies clippy unwrap_used/expect_used/panic.
MUST handle the merge_adopt / committed_root wholesale-swap path explicitly (it bypasses commit) or the index goes stale; this is the highest-risk part and MUST have a regression test.
Index rebuild on recovery MUST be crash-safe and consistent with the replayed committed root.
verification: cargo fmt --check is clean for changed files (rustfmt edition 2024; use --config skip_children=true to avoid reformatting unrelated module children).
cargo clippy -p haematite --all-targets -- -D warnings is clean (all/pedantic/nursery + unwrap/expect/panic denied).
cargo test -p haematite passes in full (lib ~365 tests + integration), zero failures.
A regression test demonstrates index correctness across commit + recovery + merge_adopt/committed-root swap (enumeration matches a full-walk oracle).
No public API change beyond what enumeration needs internally.
Behaviour-preserving for EventStore::scan (same streams, same order).

constraints.text: No file over 500 lines; extract submodules if approaching the limit.
No panics in production code; unwrap()/expect() only in tests.
mod.rs contains only pub mod / pub use; no logic.
All public types implement Debug; all error types implement std::error::Error.

resolved_context.intention: The larger haematite read-path win after PERF-001 (scan first-match) landed: make stream enumeration O(streams) not O(total entries) via a per-shard secondary index, without changing what enumeration returns. The merge_adopt / committed-root-swap path is the known trap (it bypasses the commit write-funnel). See ABLATIVE-STACK-ROADMAP.md and the haematite-perf-audit findings (audit item 'D').

provenance.quote: as fast as possible, can't have any embarrassing oversights
